wizard.name: Agent name
wizard.systemPromptHint: Tell the agent how to behave. You can use variables:
wizard.systemPromptPlaceholderLegacy: Additional instruction (optional) — the role template above takes priority; this field is a supplement.
wizard.welcomePlaceholder: Hi! How can I help you?
wizard.welcomeHint: Keep it short — one sentence works best
wizard.fallbackMessage: Fallback message (when it has no answer)
wizard.modelHint: Leave blank to use the workspace default model
wizard.successProducts: {{count}} products were automatically added to the agent
wizard.successNoProducts: No products added yet — you can add them later

settingsForm.title: Agent settings
settingsForm.deleteConfirm: Delete this agent? This cannot be undone.
settingsForm.handoffEnabled: Hand off to a human operator
settingsForm.handoffMessagePlaceholder: e.g. Connecting you to a human agent, please wait...
settingsForm.handoffKeywordsHint: Comma-separated — if the user types any of these, the conversation is escalated to a human
settingsForm.promptEngineDesc: Configure personality, tone, scope, fallback behavior, response format, and sample Q&A layer by layer. More precise and reliable than a single-line prompt.
settingsForm.roleTemplateLabel: Quick start with a role template:
settingsForm.legacyTemplatesHint: Legacy templates (free-form text field) — for backward compatibility
settingsForm.legacyTemplatesNote: When using the 6-layer engine, this field acts as a fallback.
settingsForm.assembledPrompt: Final prompt sent to the model:
settingsForm.emptyPrompt: Nothing configured yet — pick a role template or fill the layers manually.
settingsForm.personalityHint: Who are they? What traits? Describe the role and backstory.
settingsForm.toneHint: How do they speak? Formal or casual? Which words? Should match the brand voice.
settingsForm.doSayHint: Behaviors the agent must always follow.
settingsForm.dontSayHint: Forbidden behaviors — security and business guardrails.
settingsForm.fallbackHint: What to say when they don't know the answer? Never allow fabricated info.
settingsForm.qaEmpty: No examples yet — examples help the model learn the reply style.
settingsForm.customerIdentificationDesc: Ask for the customer's name and phone before answering substantive questions. Recommended for web widget; messenger channels already have identities.
settingsForm.requireCustomerInfo: Require customer identification before substantive answers
settingsForm.customerInfoPromptPlaceholder: e.g. First ask the customer's name and phone, then go into details...
settingsForm.customerInfoPromptHint: If left empty, the default instruction is used.

models.intro: Pick the model that powers your agent. Each one balances answer quality against cost differently.

channels.title: Connections
channels.subtitle: Connect your agent to your messaging apps
channels.widgetDesc: Paste this code on your website to show the agent chat
channels.widgetSettingsHint: Configure the widget's appearance, color and security
channels.allowedDomainsHint: One domain per line. Works only on these domains. Empty = anywhere.
channels.unprotectedWarning: No domain set — the widget can be embedded on any site. Add your domain to stop others spending your credits.
channels.noKeyWarning: No AI key configured — the widget cannot reply and shows a "Failed to get a response" error.
channels.subtitle: Header subtitle
channels.subtitlePh: e.g. Instant answers to your questions
channels.autoGreetHint: Auto-show the welcome message as a teaser bubble after a few seconds.
channels.leadCaptureHint: Asks for the visitor's phone before opening chat so they don't abandon mid-conversation.
channels.leadCaptureMessagePh: e.g. To help you faster, please enter your phone number.
channels.webhookWarning: Bot saved but webhook setup failed. Check the token.
channels.webhookNoInbound: No messages received yet — send one to the bot to verify the connection.
channels.botTokenPlaceholder: Paste the token from the bot provider
channels.setupGuide: How to get this — step by step
channels.webhookSetupNote: Finish setup in the Meta dashboard: open your app → Webhooks, paste the callback URL and verify token below, then subscribe to the messages and messaging fields (Instagram: also comments).
channels.fieldBotTokenPh: Paste the token from the bot provider

TELEGRAM: Choose a name and a username for your bot.
Copy the token BotFather gives you and paste it below.
BALE: Open @botfather in Bale and create a new bot.
Pick a name and username for your bot.
Copy the issued token and paste it below.
RUBIKA: Open the BotFather bot inside Rubika and create a new bot.
Set a name and username, then copy the API token.
Paste the token below. We register the receive-update endpoint automatically.
WHATSAPP: Create a Meta app at developers.facebook.com and add the WhatsApp product.
In WhatsApp → API Setup, copy the Phone number ID.
Generate a permanent access token (via a System User with whatsapp_business_messaging permission).
Paste the access token and phone number ID below and connect.
After connecting, copy the callback URL and verify token shown here into WhatsApp → Configuration → Webhook, and subscribe to the messages field.
INSTAGRAM: Convert your Instagram account to a Professional (Business/Creator) account and link it to a Facebook Page.
Create a Meta app and add the Instagram (Messenger) product.
Generate a Page access token with instagram_basic, instagram_manage_messages and instagram_manage_comments permissions.
Paste the page access token below and connect.
After connecting, copy the callback URL and verify token into the app's Webhooks, and subscribe to the messages and comments fields.

builder.title: Conversation design (flow)
builder.subtitle: Design your agent’s conversation step by step — or leave it empty to let the agent answer freely.
builder.selectHint: Select a node to edit it
builder.guideIntro: You build the conversation like a map: start from “Start” and connect nodes in order.
builder.guideStep1: Pick a node from the side panel to add it to the canvas.
builder.guideStep2: Drag from a node’s edge handle to the next node to build the path.
builder.guideStep3: Click any node to edit its text and settings in the side panel.
builder.guideNote: Note: building a flow is optional. Without one, the agent answers intelligently from your instructions and knowledge. Use a flow only when you want a precise, controlled path.
builder.templatesNote: Picking a template replaces the current canvas. You can edit or delete any node afterwards.
builder.emptyCanvas: Canvas is empty — add a node from the side panel
builder.connectHint: To connect, drag from a node’s edge handle to the next node
builder.descMessage: Send a fixed message to the user
builder.descCondition: Branch the path based on a condition
builder.descCollect: Ask for and collect info from the user (e.g. name or phone)
builder.descProduct: Look up products and present them
builder.descHandoff: Hand the conversation to a human agent

bubbles.text: I'm looking for running shoes in size 42
We have some great options! Here's our best seller:

product.name: Pro Runner X42
product.desc: Pro running shoe with shock-absorbing sole and lightweight design

bubbles.text: Available in black, white and navy. Black is our most popular.
I heard you — reserved a pair of black size 42 for you. I'll hold it for 24 hours.
I'm sorry for the delay. What's your order number?
Your order is being packed — it ships tomorrow morning and arrives by the day after.
Unfortunately that's not possible for today. I'll hand this off to a human teammate to help you.
I need a laptop for programming
Based on your needs, I recommend this model:

product.name: MacBook Pro 14"
product.desc: M3 Pro chip, 18GB RAM, ideal for dev and design

bubbles.text: Yes, 3 units in stock. Same-day delivery for Tehran.
18 months official manufacturer warranty + 7-day test and return period.

items.quote: Our Telegram support is fully automated now. The agent answers product questions instantly, day and night.
items.name: Sara M.

items.quote: Setup took ten minutes. Uploading our catalog and connecting Bale was effortless.
items.name: Reza K.

items.quote: Bringing our own OpenRouter key means we control the costs. Exactly what we needed.
items.name: Niloofar A.

items.q: Do I need technical skills to use it?
items.a: No. Create an agent in a few clicks, upload your data, and add a connection.

items.q: How are AI token costs handled?
items.a: You bring your own OpenRouter key, and token costs are billed directly to your account. Vigent only charges for the platform.
Telegram, WhatsApp, Instagram, Rubika, Bale and a web widget — all in one dashboard.

items.q: Can I try it for free?
items.a: Yes — a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

cta.note: No credit card required — just your phone number
